title: EARLY CHILDHOOD PROGRAMS - 211info
text: OREGON COMMUNITY PROGRAMS EARLY CHILDHOOD PROGRAMS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides intensive family therapy, including a behavioral management program focused on reducing challenging or difficult behaviors like, verbal or physical aggression, symptoms associated with PTSD, and emotional distress. 1170 Pearl Street Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Ages 0-6 and referred by ODHS or Oregon Youth Authority Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 743-4340 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 743-4340 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or visit website for more information FEES: Accepts OHP DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Lane County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located on Lane Transit District downtown loop bus route, corner of 12th and Pearl. Services: Individual Counseling Parent Child Interactive Therapy Other Services Offered At This Location: COUNSELING SETTINGS - CONNECTIONS PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SUPPORT & THERAPY GROUPS MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - INTENSIVE IN-HOME BEHAVIORAL HEALTH TREATMENT MUTUAL SUPPORT - FOSTER PARENT SUPPORT GROUP If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
